story.iWant: to compile packages with multiple `.pg` files
story.soThat: pipeline definitions can be organized across files within the same package
story.tasks:
- **Task 1: Extend Parser for Multi-File Context** (AC: Phase 2 implementation) - 1.1: Add multi-file compilation context to Parser struct - 1.2: Implement file discovery within same package - 1.3: Parse and validate `[#]` file ordering markers - 1.4: Store file ordering metadata in parser state
- **Task 2: Implement Phase 2 Resolution Logic** (AC: Three-phase resolution) - 2.1: Extend `resolve_pipeline_reference` with Phase 2 search - 2.2: Search same-package files in `[#]` order - 2.3: Identify same package by matching `PackageSpec` (registry, path, version) - 2.4: Cache parsed files to avoid re-parsing
- **Task 3: File Ordering Validation** (AC: File ordering validation) - 3.1: Detect duplicate `[#]` markers across package files - 3.2: Implement `ParserError::DuplicateFileOrder` with file paths - 3.3: Validate sequential ordering and warn on gaps - 3.4: Handle files without `[#]` markers (process last)
- **Task 4: Integration Testing** (AC: Integration tests) - 4.1: Test pipeline call from `[#] 1` → `[#] 2` (should succeed) - 4.2: Test pipeline call from `[#] 2` → `[#] 1` (should fail) - 4.3: Test files without `[#]` can call numbered files - 4.4: Test duplicate `[#]` detection - 4.5: Test multi-file package compilation end-to-end
- **Task 5: Error Messages Enhancement** (AC: Error clarity) - 5.1: Include file paths in all multi-file errors - 5.2: Provide suggestions for resolution order issues - 5.3: Clear error messages for undefined pipeline references

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
**Given** multiple `.pg` files in the same package **When** I compile the package **Then** Phase 2 resolution searches same-package files before external registry
**And** file ordering is controlled by `[#]` markers: - Files can specify compilation order: `[#] 1`, `[#] 2`, etc. - Files without `[#]` markers are processed after numbered files - Duplicate `[#]` numbers raise `ParserError::DuplicateFileOrder`
**And** three-phase resolution is fully implemented: 1. **Phase 1:** Current file namespace 2. **Phase 2:** Same package, different files (by `[#]` order) 3. **Phase 3:** External packages (registry/database)
**And** file ordering validation: - Detect duplicate `[#]` markers across package files - Report error with both file paths containing duplicate number - Validate ordering is sequential (warn if gaps like 1, 3, 5)
**And** integration tests verify: - Pipeline in file `[#] 1` can be called from file `[#] 2` - Pipeline in file `[#] 2` cannot be called from file `[#] 1` (compilation error) - Files without `[#]` markers can call pipelines from numbered files - Duplicate `[#]` markers detected and reported - Multi-file package compiles correctly

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: Polyglot eliminates the FFI integration tax by handling cross-language type conversion at runtime. This enables developers to call functions across languages without manual FFI setup, reducing 4-6 hour integrations to 15 minutes.

doc.title: Architecture Overview
doc.snippet: Polyglot pipelines are automation jobs triggered by events. Each pipeline is defined in .pg files with boxes connected by I/O. The architecture supports multi-file packages where pipelines can reference each other across files within the same package.

doc.title: Epic 1: Lexer & Parser
doc.snippet: Three-phase resolution strategy: Phase 1 (current file namespace), Phase 2 (same package, different files by [#] order), Phase 3 (external packages via registry). File ordering markers [#] N control compilation order with validation for duplicates.

artifact.reason: Main parser implementation from Story 1.5. Needs extension with multi-file context (package_files field) and Phase 2 resolution logic in pipeline reference handling.
Phase 3 resolution implementation. Provides pattern to follow for Phase 2 file-based resolution - uses ImportResolver trait for consistency.
Comprehensive error types (26+ variants). Needs new DuplicateFileOrder variant with file paths for multi-file validation errors.
AST structures for package identification and pipeline definitions. PackageSpec (registry, path, version) used to identify same-package files.
Test infrastructure established in Story 1.5. Pattern to follow for multi-file integration tests with fixtures in tests/fixtures/.
Resolution trait interface. Phase 2 resolver should follow same pattern for consistency with Phase 3 (FileRegistryResolver).

rust.package: Lexer crate (Story 1.3) - tokenization input for parser
Error handling - used for ParserError enum derive
JSON serialization - used by FileRegistryResolver for registry files

constraints.constraint: Must follow parser patterns established in Story 1.5: token stream navigation with skip_newlines(), comprehensive span tracking, clone tokens to avoid borrow checker issues
Use ImportResolver trait pattern for consistency - Phase 2 resolution should mirror FileRegistryResolver architecture
Extend ParserError enum (error.rs) with new DuplicateFileOrder variant including both file paths
Maintain AST structure using enum variants (TypeAnnotation::Named, Identifier::Pipeline, etc.)
Cache parsed files in HashMap<PathBuf, Program> to avoid re-parsing same files during multi-file compilation
Error messages must include file paths for all multi-file errors (clarity requirement from AC)
Integration tests must use fixtures in polyglot-parser/tests/fixtures/ following Story 1.5 pattern
Performance: Phase 2 search limited to same-package files only, stop at first match

interface.name: ImportResolver trait
Parser::parse() entry point
interface.signature: pub fn parse(mut self) -> Result<Program, ParserError>

interface.name: PackageSpec for same-package identification
ParserError enum (to extend)

tests.standards: Story 1.5 established comprehensive testing infrastructure with 87 tests passing (83 unit + 4 integration). Testing standards include: - Unit tests in polyglot-parser/src/tests.rs for parser logic - Integration tests in polyglot-parser/tests/integration_tests.rs using realistic .pg fixtures - Test fixtures stored in polyglot-parser/tests/fixtures/ directory - Use FileRegistryResolver::empty() for tests without registry dependencies - Test both success and error cases with comprehensive error checking - Verify AST structure correctness and span tracking - Follow Rust testing conventions with descriptive test names
tests.locations: - polyglot-parser/src/tests.rs - Unit tests for parser methods - polyglot-parser/tests/integration_tests.rs - End-to-end integration tests - polyglot-parser/tests/fixtures/ - Test .pg files and registry JSON

test-idea.name: test_pipeline_call_forward_reference_succeeds
test-idea.description: Create fixture with [#] 1 and [#] 2 files. Pipeline in [#] 1 should be callable from [#] 2 (forward reference allowed).

test-idea.name: test_pipeline_call_backward_reference_fails
test-idea.description: Create fixture with [#] 1 and [#] 2 files. Pipeline in [#] 2 should NOT be callable from [#] 1 (backward reference blocked).

test-idea.name: test_unnumbered_files_call_numbered_files
test-idea.description: Files without [#] markers (processed last) should be able to call pipelines from numbered files.

test-idea.name: test_duplicate_file_order_detection
test-idea.description: Two files with same [#] number should raise ParserError::DuplicateFileOrder with both file paths.

test-idea.name: test_sequential_ordering_gap_warning
test-idea.description: Files with gaps in ordering ([#] 1, [#] 3, [#] 5) should emit warning about non-sequential ordering.

test-idea.name: test_multi_file_package_compilation_end_to_end
test-idea.description: Complete multi-file package with imports, pipelines, and cross-file references compiles successfully.

test-idea.name: test_resolution_order_phase_1_2_3
test-idea.description: Verify resolution tries: (1) current file, (2) same-package files, (3) external registry - in that order.

test-idea.name: test_same_package_identified_by_package_spec
test-idea.description: Files with matching PackageSpec (registry, path, version) identified as same package.
